hypothesis: If a convolutional neural network is first pretrained on a data-rich domain of additive-free lithium-ion battery SEM images and then fine-tuned on a data-poor domain of additive-containing samples, then this two-stage transfer learning approach will achieve higher classification accuracy across NCM compositions and aging states compared to fine-tuning without pretraining or pretraining alone, because the source domain pretraining enables the model to learn transferable morphological features that compensate for the scarcity of labeled target domain data.
